history.notes: Massive 'dopamine detox 2026' trend across major news outlets and TikTok; few dedicated iOS apps in this niche despite high search volume.
Advancing to research — spawning Validator. NOTE: Very similar to killed concept 'detox-day' — validator should compare.

KILL: Near-identical to detox-day (killed same day) — same 10+ saturated competitor field (Opal $10M ARR, One Sec, Forest, 7+ dedicated detox apps), same Apple Screen Time competition (documented failure pattern), same elevated App Store review risk (2019 crackdown). 'Guided recovery' reframe does not constitute meaningful differentiation — Doxo AI already occupies that positioning. MRR math unchanged at low ($60-$400/mo).

killReasons: Near-duplicate of killed concept 'detox-day' — validator rules require kill unless situation has materially changed, and it has not (killed same day).

Competitor saturation exceeds threshold (10+): Opal ($10M ARR, 4.8 stars), One Sec (4.83 stars, 22K ratings), Forest (millions of users), Dopamine Detox: Restrict Apps, Elqi, Zario, Doxo AI, AppBlock, Cleverest, FocusZone, plus Apple Screen Time built-in.
Directly competes with Apple's built-in Screen Time — scout learnings explicitly flag 'apps competing directly with Apple's built-in apps' as a failure pattern.
Apple has history of removing/restricting third-party screen time apps (2019 crackdown, 2020 Congressional hearing, EU antitrust complaints). Elevated App Store review risk for solo indie developer.
'Guided recovery' positioning is not differentiated — Doxo AI already brands as 'Dopamine Detox Coach' with guided approach.

demandSignals: Multiple news outlets covering dopamine detox trend (ActionNews5, WNEM, WFMZ)
Google Trends 2026 wellness reports list digital detox as key trend
marketSize.notes: Demand is real and growing, but demand alone is insufficient — identical finding to detox-day. The $1.12B digital detox app market is already captured by well-funded incumbents.

competitors.identifiedGap: None. The 'guided recovery' angle that dopamine-reset proposes is already occupied by Doxo AI (Dopamine Detox Coach). Opal covers gamification and beautiful design. No viable gap remains.
competitors.notes: Exceeds max_competitor_saturation threshold of 10. Same competitor landscape as detox-day kill assessment from earlier today.

monetization.mrrMath: Saturated market → 500-1000 downloads/mo realistic. At 3% conversion to $2.99/wk ($12.87/mo effective) = 15-30 subscribers = $193-$386/mo MRR. Even at optimistic 2000 downloads/mo at 5% = $1,287/mo — but weekly churn is high (50%+ monthly), netting ~$640/mo best case. Does not reliably reach medium threshold.
monetization.notes: Weekly subscription model has higher churn than monthly. Users who want free use Apple Screen Time. Users who want premium use Opal. No viable price positioning for a new entrant.

risks: Without app blocking (requires Family Controls entitlement with Apple approval), the app is just a timer with challenges — extremely undifferentiated
Family Controls entitlement historically difficult to obtain for indie developers
technicalFeasibility.notes: Basic timer/streak/challenge features are buildable in 1 week with SwiftUI. But without app blocking capability, the core value proposition is weak.

concerns: Apple removed/restricted multiple third-party screen time apps in 2019
Congressional and EU antitrust scrutiny of Apple's behavior in this category
Family Controls entitlement required for blocking features — Apple controls the gate
Category directly overlaps with Apple's built-in Screen Time
appStoreRisk.notes: Identical risk profile to detox-day. Solo indie developer cannot weather App Store enforcement actions that well-funded companies like Opal can.

comparisonToDetoxDay.differentiationAnalysis: dopamine-reset reframes 'digital detox' as 'guided dopamine recovery with science-backed timelines.' This is a messaging change, not a product change. Doxo AI already positions as a guided dopamine detox coach. The underlying market dynamics (saturation, Apple competition, review risk) are identical and unchanged.
